From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1970 studio album by Billy Butler

| Yesterday, Today & Tomorrow | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Studio album](/wiki/Album "Album") by [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)") | |
| Released | 1970 |
| Recorded | April 27 and June 29, 1970 |
| Studio | [Van Gelder Studio](/wiki/Van_Gelder_Studio "Van Gelder Studio"), Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Length | 33:03 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") PR 7797 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Bob Porter](/wiki/Bob_Porter_(record_producer) "Bob Porter (record producer)") |
| [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[Guitar Soul!](/wiki/Guitar_Soul! "Guitar Soul!")* (1969) | ***Yesterday, Today & Tomorrow*** (1970) | *[Night Life](/wiki/Night_Life_(Billy_Butler_album) "Night Life (Billy Butler album)")* (1971) | | |

***Yesterday, Today & Tomorrow*** is the third album led by guitarist [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)") which was recorded in 1970 and released on the [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") label.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yesterday,_Today_%26_Tomorrow_(Billy_Butler_album)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") | StarStarStarStar[[2]](#cite_note-Allmusic-2) |

[Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") awarded the album 8 stars stating "Billy Butler was a guitarist's guitarist and an innovator in both production and arrangements. This disc is solid from top to bottom and reveals the restless spirit of a quiet yet decomposing artist".[[2]](#cite_note-Allmusic-2)

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yesterday,_Today_%26_Tomorrow_(Billy_Butler_album)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

All compositions by Billy Butler except where noted

1. "Yesterday, Today and Tomorrow" - 5:14
2. "[Girl Talk](/wiki/Girl_Talk_(Neal_Hefti_song) "Girl Talk (Neal Hefti song)")" ([Neal Hefti](/wiki/Neal_Hefti "Neal Hefti"), [Bobby Troup](/wiki/Bobby_Troup "Bobby Troup")) - 4:57
3. "[Dancing on the Ceiling](/wiki/Dancing_on_the_Ceiling_(1930_song) "Dancing on the Ceiling (1930 song)")" ([Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart"), [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers")) - 5:45
4. "Hold It!" (Billy Butler, Clifford Scott) - 3:52
5. "Evening Dreams" - 4:11
6. "The Butler Did It" - 3:49
7. "[Sweet Georgia Brown](/wiki/Sweet_Georgia_Brown "Sweet Georgia Brown")" ([Ben Bernie](/wiki/Ben_Bernie "Ben Bernie"), [Kenneth Casey](/wiki/Kenneth_Casey "Kenneth Casey"), [Maceo Pinkard](/wiki/Maceo_Pinkard "Maceo Pinkard")) - 4:48

* Recorded at Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey on April 27 (tracks 5 & 6), and June 29 (tracks 1–4 & 7), 1970

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yesterday,_Today_%26_Tomorrow_(Billy_Butler_album)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)") - [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar"), [bass guitar](/wiki/Bass_guitar "Bass guitar")
* [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person") - [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone") (tracks 1–4 & 7)
* [Jerome Richardson](/wiki/Jerome_Richardson "Jerome Richardson") - tenor saxophone, flute (tracks 5 & 6)
* Ernie Hayes (tracks 1–4 & 7), [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") (tracks 5 & 6) - [organ](/wiki/Electronic_organ "Electronic organ"), [electric piano](/wiki/Electric_piano "Electric piano")
* [Everett Barksdale](/wiki/Everett_Barksdale "Everett Barksdale"), Billy Suyker - guitar (tracks 5 & 6)
* [Jimmy Lewis](/wiki/Jimmy_Lewis_(bassist) "Jimmy Lewis (bassist)") (tracks 1–4 & 7), [Chuck Rainey](/wiki/Chuck_Rainey "Chuck Rainey") (tracks 5 & 6) - [electric bass](/wiki/Electric_bass "Electric bass")
* Jimmy Johnson - [drums](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit")
